text: Before the deadline, I kept refreshing my email every few minutes and could not focus on a single task. My body felt tightly wound.
I lay in bed replaying tomorrow’s conversation and imagining everything going wrong. Sleep would not come because my thoughts would not slow down.
I feel restless and keyed up, like I need to move but have nowhere to put the energy.
Even small choices feel high-stakes right now, and I keep second-guessing myself.
My breathing gets shallow whenever I open my to-do list, and my attention scatters immediately.
I keep checking details repeatedly because I am afraid of missing something important.
I feel on edge all day, startled by notifications and unable to settle into calm focus.
My mind runs fast from one worry to the next, and it is hard to feel safe in the present moment.
I am tense in my shoulders and jaw, with a constant background hum of worry.
I feel mentally crowded by what-if scenarios, and none of them end well.
I cannot relax enough to enjoy the evening because my thoughts keep looping around possible mistakes.
I feel jittery and overactivated, as if I drank too much coffee even when I did not.
I keep looking for reassurance, but the anxiety returns within minutes.
I am worried without a clear endpoint, and my body stays braced for bad news.
My internal state is tense and vigilant, with very little sense of ease.
